skill.name: cc-skill-frontend-patterns
skill.description: Frontend development patterns for React, Next.js, state management, performance optimization, accessibility, and UI best practices.
skill.summary: Use practical React and Next.js frontend patterns for maintainable, performant user interfaces.
skill.author: affaan-m

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged dynamic imports, fetch calls, backticks, key-related text, weak crypto patterns, and a critical heuristic. Review found these are Markdown TypeScript examples for frontend development, not executable skill code. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown code fences or TypeScript template literals in examples. They do not execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is documentation with fenced TypeScript examples. No Ruby, shell interpreter, or command execution wrapper exists in the skill.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Dynamic Import Examples
low_findings.description: The dynamic import findings show React lazy loading of local components. They are frontend examples, not hidden runtime loading by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The imports are inside a code splitting example in SKILL.md. They reference local component paths and are not executed by the skill.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Fetch Examples
low_findings.description: The fetch findings are educational React data-loading examples. They do not send secrets or contact a suspicious external endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The visible endpoints are a prop value and a local /api path. I found no evidence of credential collection or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Key Text Is Not Credential Access
low_findings.description: The key-related findings are object key inspection, keyboard event handling, and React event logic. No certificate or key file access is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is form validation and keyboard navigation. I found no filenames, PEM data, or secret-reading behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Crypto Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak crypto hits occur around normal words such as description. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are metadata or UI form examples. There is no crypto operation to misuse.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance hits are React state names, class names, or callback options. They do not inspect the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is UI state and async hook options. I found no operating system probes, environment dumps, or discovery commands.

low_findings.title: Critical Heuristic Dismissed After Context Review
low_findings.description: The dangerous combination was produced by false-positive code execution, network, and credential-access signals in Markdown examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All cited evidence is within the only scanned Markdown file. No executable companion files or prompt injection text were found.

content.value_statement: Frontend teams need repeatable patterns that keep React interfaces maintainable and fast. This skill provides practical examples for components, hooks, state, performance, forms, animation, and accessibility.
actual_capabilities: Explains component composition, compound components, and render props.
Provides custom hook patterns for toggles, async queries, and debounced values.
Shows React state management with context and reducers.
Documents performance patterns such as memoization, lazy loading, and virtualization.
Covers forms, error boundaries, animations, keyboard navigation, and focus management.
limitations: It is documentation, not an executable frontend framework.
Examples use placeholder domain models and need project-specific adaptation.
It does not include automated tests or lint rules.
It does not cover backend APIs, deployment, or database design.

use_cases.title: Standardize React Patterns
use_cases.description: Create consistent component and hook conventions for a growing product codebase.

use_cases.title: Improve Interface Performance
use_cases.description: Apply memoization, lazy loading, and list virtualization patterns to reduce unnecessary rendering.

use_cases.title: Build Accessible Interactions
use_cases.description: Use keyboard navigation and focus management examples when implementing menus, modals, and forms.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Component Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to recommend a React component pattern for my feature. Explain the tradeoffs and show a concise implementation plan.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a Custom Hook
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my hook design. Suggest changes for state, effects, callback stability, and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Slow View
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to identify React performance patterns for this screen. Include memoization, lazy loading, and virtualization where relevant.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Accessible Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design keyboard, focus, validation, and error patterns for a complex modal workflow.

output_examples.input: Review a tab interface pattern for a dashboard.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend a compound component structure with shared context, explicit tab IDs, and clear provider boundaries.

output_examples.input: Improve rendering for a long market list.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest virtualization, stable row sizing, limited overscan, and memoized item rendering.
The skill would cover focus capture, focus restoration, Escape handling, dialog roles, and visible validation feedback.

best_practices: Adapt the examples to your existing component and state conventions.
Pair performance patterns with measurement before and after the change.
Check accessibility behavior with keyboard and screen reader testing.
anti_patterns: Do not add memoization everywhere without a measured rendering problem.
Do not copy placeholder API calls without validating data and error states.
Do not use animation patterns that block keyboard or focus behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete applications?
faq.answer: No. It provides frontend patterns and examples that an assistant can adapt to your application.

faq.question: Can it help with Next.js projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The React patterns are useful in Next.js, but routing and server features need project-specific guidance.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: Only indirectly. It covers frontend structure, validation, and error handling, not a full security review.

faq.question: Are the examples production ready?
faq.answer: They are reference patterns. You should add typing, tests, styling, and edge cases for your product.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I provide when using it?
faq.answer: Provide the component goal, current code, performance issue, or accessibility workflow you want to improve.
